description_long: This object consists of eight rustic cabins arranged in a circular formation around a central open wooden platform. Each cabin features a thatched, hipped roof and walls constructed from natural wood planks, giving a traditional, handcrafted appearance. The cabins are hexagonal in shape, with small, evenly spaced rectangular windows and single doorways facing the central communal area. The roofs are steeply pitched and extend slightly beyond the walls, providing some overhang for weather protection. The central platform is made of wooden planks laid in a radial pattern, connecting all cabins and creating a shared gathering space. The overall structure evokes a communal village or encampment, suitable for rural or park-like urban settings.
description: A circular cluster of eight rustic wooden cabins with thatched hipped roofs, arranged around a central wooden platform. Each cabin has small windows and a single door facing the communal area.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrances of the nearest cabins and the central wooden platform.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view revealing more of the side walls and windows of the cabins, with the central platform still prominent.
description_view_2: Rear-angled view displaying the back and side walls of the cabins, with less emphasis on the entrances.
description_view_3: Side view highlighting the lateral arrangement of the cabins and the continuity of the central platform.
attribute_roof_type: hipped thatched roof with moderate overhangs, constructed from natural plant material
attribute_window_arrangement: Each cabin has three small, rectangular windows symmetrically distributed on the visible facades, with one window on each of the three main sides facing outward from the central platform.
attribute_door_placement: Single door centered on the facade of each cabin facing the central communal platform.
